reasoning: The baseline probability for a stock to be higher on a future date has an empirical base rate of ~0.576. For KMB, an established, low-volatility consumer staple, the short-term probability of being higher than the current value aligns with this base rate. Following the prompt's instruction to increase uncertainty (move closer to 0.5) for distant forecast horizons, the probabilities are monotonically reduced towards 0.5. Fundamentally, this is justified because KMB is a dividend-paying stock; over a 3-month horizon, it will encounter an ex-dividend date which drops the share price naturally, reducing the technical probability that the nominal stock price is strictly higher over longer intervals within a quarter.
